name: Belmont Housing Resources for Wny, Incorporated
description_short: Non-profit housing counselor providing HUD-certified counseling, affordable housing assistance, and financial education to low-income families across Western New York.
description_long:
Belmont Housing Resources for WNY is a non-profit organization dedicated to strengthening families and communities throughout Western New York by providing affordable and accessible housing solutions. Founded on the principle that stable housing builds stronger communities, the organization serves over 15,000 low-income households annually across Erie, Niagara, and Chautauqua Counties.

The organization offers a comprehensive range of services including HUD-certified housing and financial counseling at no cost, administration of the Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) program generating over $35 million in annual rental assistance, homebuyer education workshops, financial coaching, foreclosure prevention counseling, and property management of affordable rental units. They also provide housing rehabilitation services, landlord education, and community development programs that leverage approximately $10 million in affordable housing development funds annually while administering $10.2 million in community development programs.

Belmont distinguishes itself through its multi-faceted approach combining counseling with direct housing development and asset management of $73.1 million. The organization provides free, professional guidance on rental, financial, homebuying, and foreclosure prevention matters, staffed by HUD-certified counselors. They actively develop and rehabilitate affordable housing units and manage rental properties, moving beyond counseling to create tangible housing opportunities for first-time homebuyers and low-income renters.

As a legitimate non-profit with established partnerships and funders, Belmont represents a trustworthy resource for consumers seeking housing assistance, financial education, and counseling without commercial lending obligations. The client testimonials reflect genuine satisfaction with their respectful, supportive approach. However, as a regional organization limited to Western New York, their services are geographically restricted, and like most non-profits, their availability may be constrained by funding and staffing limitations.
pros: HUD-certified housing and financial counselors providing free, professional guidance
Administers Housing Choice Voucher program generating $35M+ in annual rental assistance
Offers multiple free educational workshops including homebuyer education and financial literacy
Actively develops and rehabilitates affordable housing, not just providing counseling
Manages $73.1 million in assets and leverages $10M+ annually in housing development funds
Serves over 15,000 low-income households annually across three counties
Provides specialized counseling for foreclosure prevention, HECM, and post-purchase situations
cons: Limited to Western New York region (Erie, Niagara, and Chautauqua Counties) only
As a non-profit, service availability may be constrained by funding and staff capacity
No indication of evening or weekend counseling hours for working individuals
Website lacks specific information about average wait times for counseling appointments
Limited details on digital/remote counseling options despite serving multiple counties
best_for: Low-income renters and homebuyers in Western New York seeking affordable housing solutions
First-time homebuyers needing HUD-certified counseling and educational workshops
Individuals at risk of foreclosure seeking prevention counseling and financial guidance
Landlords seeking education, property management services, and compliance guidance
diagnosis: Belmont is best for low-income individuals and families in Western New York seeking free, professional HUD-certified housing counseling, homebuyer education, and rental assistance programs. The primary limitation is geographic restriction to Erie, Niagara, and Chautauqua Counties, making services unavailable to consumers outside this region.
services: Financial Coaching and Financial Education Workshops
Foreclosure Prevention and Mortgage Help Counseling
Property Management of Affordable Rental Units
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: Non-profit housing counselor providing HUD-certified counseling and financial education to low-income families in Western New York.
